Records.description: Triangle ladder family, triangle-ladder_L8_massless-internal-offshell-external. The Usyukina-Davydychev triangle ladder $T^{(L)}$ consists of $(L{-}1)$ box cells capped by a triangle at the apex, with two rails of $L$ edges converging to a shared apex vertex and $L$ rungs connecting the rails at each level. It has $2L{+}1$ vertices, $3L$ massless internal propagators, $L$ loops, and three off-shell external legs at the two base vertices and the apex. The triangle ladder evaluates to the same $\Phi^{(L)}$ as the four-point box ladder and the generalised-ladder fan via the conformal magic identity, but is a genuinely different topology from both for $L \ge 2$: the triangle ladder has $V = 2L{+}1$, $E = 3L$ versus $V = L{+}2$, $E = 2L{+}1$ for the fan. Only $L = 1$ (the triangle) is shared. Value = PhiLadder[8, 1/3, 1/5].
